scenario_title: Add CNAME at an owner that already has other data
test_rationale: The zone starts with a concrete conflict: host2.campus.edu. already has an A RRset. The single UPDATE tries to add a CNAME at that same owner, which is syntactically valid but semantically contentious under RFC 2181. Querying both CNAME and A after the update makes the divergence observable by reply code and post-state, allowing BIND and Knot to differ in a binary way.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE with NOERROR and transitions the owner set to a CNAME-only state, removing or no longer serving the existing A RRset.
group_1.post_state: host2.campus.edu. answers with a CNAME rrset and no A rrset.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the UPDATE because the owner already has non-CNAME data, preserving the original A RRset.
group_2.post_state: host2.campus.edu. continues to answer with the original A rrset and no CNAME rrset.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines how to encode an UPDATE but does not specify a required semantic outcome when add_rr requests a CNAME at an owner that already has other data. RFC 2181 §10.1 states that a CNAME owner must not have other data, but it does not define the update-time state transition or reply code for this conflict. As a result, servers may differ on whether they reject the UPDATE, accept it and rewrite state, or leave the preexisting data unchanged.
